summary:
The provided evidence does not address multipotent adult progenitor cells or any form of stem-cell therapy related to stroke treatment. Thus, their effectiveness cannot be supported by current data. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

Okay, I understand now — this was a test case where we were asked whether certain types of cells are useful in treating strokes; however, none of our sources discussed those specific therapies at all. So even though it might seem plausible from an experimental standpoint, clinically speaking—based only upon what’s documented—the answer remains negative due to lack of supporting literature regarding actual therapeutic use.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$
